words.example_english: Don't hang out with him.

words.example_english: I paid cash for the car.

words.example_italian: Tom non si presenta mai in orario.
words.example_english: Tom never shows up on time.

words.example_italian: Abito a casa di mio zio.
words.example_english: I live in my uncle's house.

words.example_italian: Aveva la competenza che cercavo disperatamente.
words.example_english: He had the expertise I was looking for desperately.

words.example_italian: Io non riesco a sentirti bene.
words.example_english: I can't hear you well.

words.example_italian: Facciamo un patto.
words.example_english: Let's make a pact.

words.example_italian: Tom è stato preso in custodia.
words.example_english: Tom was taken into custody.

words.example_italian: Mi sono svegliato all'alba.
words.example_english: I woke up at dawn.

words.example_italian: I miei colleghi mi accolsero calorosamente.
words.example_english: My colleagues warmly welcomed me.

words.example_italian: Togliti le calze, per favore.
words.example_english: Take off your socks, please.

words.example_italian: È sicuro di non volere nulla?
words.example_english: Are you sure you don't want anything?

words.example_italian: Io ti ho dato troppe opportunità.
words.example_english: I've given you too many chances.

words.example_italian: Lui pesa più di ottanta chili.
words.example_english: He is over 80 kilos.

words.example_italian: Il suo amico è un ex Senatore.
words.example_english: His friend is an ex-Senator.

words.example_italian: Tom è un artista di strada.
words.example_english: Tom is a street artist.

words.example_italian: Io suono il piano per divertimento.
words.example_english: I play the piano for amusement.

words.example_italian: Dà troppi soldi a suo figlio.
words.example_english: She gives too much money to her son.

words.example_italian: Lui berrà lo champagne a mezzanotte.
words.example_english: He will drink the champagne at midnight.

words.example_italian: Ti ho detto di non muoverti.
words.example_english: I told you not to move.

words.example_italian: Tom se ne andò a mezzanotte.
words.example_english: Tom left at midnight.

words.example_italian: Lui ha i privilegi da socio.
words.example_english: He has the privileges of membership.

words.example_italian: Tu ti sbagli su di me.
words.example_english: You're wrong about me.

words.example_italian: Tom è più esperto di me.
words.example_english: Tom is more experienced than me.

words.example_italian: Non è un po' in anticipo?
words.example_english: Aren't you a little early?

words.example_italian: Il gatto scappa via.
words.example_english: The cat runs away.

words.example_italian: Io ho troppe cose da fare.
words.example_english: I've got too much to do.

words.example_italian: Ti sarei grato se lo facessi.
words.example_english: I'd appreciate it if you could do that.

words.example_italian: Mi serve la tua firma qui.
words.example_english: I need your signature here.

words.example_italian: Mi sento in cima al mondo.
words.example_english: I feel on top of the world.

words.example_italian: Il caso non è stato risolto.
words.example_english: The case hasn't been solved.

words.example_italian: Sto provando a tenerlo in vita.
words.example_english: I'm trying to keep him alive.

words.example_italian: L'intelligenza artificiale farà sparire questi lavori?
words.example_english: Will artificial intelligence make these jobs disappear?

words.example_italian: Perché non usciamo a bere stasera?
words.example_english: Why don't we go out drinking tonight?

words.example_italian: Non posso vedere il mio babbo.
words.example_english: I can't see my dad.

words.example_italian: Non ci sono più posti liberi.
words.example_english: There are no more seats left.

words.example_italian: Tu te lo meriti il premio.
words.example_english: You deserve the prize.

words.example_italian: State calmi e comportatevi bene!
words.example_english: Keep quiet and behave well!

words.example_italian: Bevi meno e dormi di più.
words.example_english: Drink less and sleep more.

words.example_italian: A me non va di festeggiare.
words.example_english: I don't feel like partying.

words.example_italian: Ti piace il rock and roll?
words.example_english: Do you like rock and roll?

words.example_italian: Qual è il tuo bersaglio?
words.example_english: Which is your target?

words.example_italian: Tom spese il doppio di me.
words.example_english: Tom spent twice as much as I did.

words.example_italian: So che si preoccupa per lei.
words.example_english: I know you worry about her.
